message:
So I've become that person. You know the one—the one whose car trunk has a go-bag that's basically a small apartment, and whose hall closet looks like a bug-out boutique. My kids used to roll their eyes. Last month, when a water main broke on our street, three neighbors showed up asking if I had extra bottles. Suddenly I went from "quirky" to "the prepared one," and I'm not gonna lie, it felt pretty good.

Here's the thing though: I didn't wake up one day deciding to be that person. It was just... one small thing at a time. A sturdy bag here, a few gallons of water there, documents in a folder instead of scattered. Nothing dramatic or doomsday-ish. Just the kind of stuff that makes sense when you think about it.

I'm not saying you need to become me (thank goodness, right?), but that version of yourself who takes one afternoon to get some basics sorted? That person is kind of wonderful.

What's one prep thing that surprised you by actually coming in handy?
